CREATE TABLE test_repeat AS SELECT repeat_row(1, 2, 'foo', num_rows = 3);
CREATE TABLE repeat_rows AS SELECT * FROM repeat_row(1, 5, 'bar', num_rows = 10);

CREATE TABLE complex_test AS SELECT x, y FROM (SELECT repeat_row(2, 3, 'baz', num_rows = 5)) AS subquery JOIN (SELECT repeat_row(3, 3, 'qux', num_rows = 4)) AS subquery2 ON subquery.col1 = subquery2.col1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT repeat_row(1, 2, 'test', num_rows = 100) UNION SELECT repeat_row(2, 2, 'test2', num_rows = 100)) AS combined;
CREATE TABLE range_test AS SELECT repeat_row(i, 1, 'row', num_rows = (i + 3)) FROM (SELECT 1 AS i UNION SELECT 2 UNION SELECT 3);
SELECT * FROM (SELECT repeat_row('a', 1, 2, 'foo', num_rows = 20) AS value1, repeat_row('b', 1, 5, 'bar', num_rows = 15) AS value2) AS combined;
CREATE TABLE final_test AS SELECT repeat_row('value1', 1, 'value2', num_rows = (CASE WHEN 1 > 0 THEN 5 ELSE 1 END));
WITH repeat_cte AS (SELECT repeat_row('row', 1, 1, num_rows = 2) AS r) SELECT COUNT(*) FROM repeat_cte;
CREATE TABLE join_test AS SELECT a.foo, b.bar FROM (SELECT repeat_row(1, 2, 'foo', num_rows = 2) AS foo) AS a JOIN (SELECT repeat_row(2, 2, 'bar', num_rows = 2) AS bar) AS b ON a.foo = b.bar;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT repeat_row(1, 1, 'test', num_rows = 50) INTERSECT SELECT repeat_row(1, 1, 'test', num_rows = 50)) AS intersection;
